description_long: This flower features a prominent, pouch-like lower petal in a soft pink hue, characteristic of the Paphiopedilum or 'lady slipper' orchid. The upper sepal is large, rounded, and pale green with dark maroon spots, arching protectively over the central structure. Two lateral petals extend horizontally, displaying a gradient from pink to maroon and a slightly wavy texture. The central pouch is bulbous and smooth, forming a distinct cup shape. The flower lacks visible leaves or stem in these views, focusing attention on its unique floral morphology. The overall appearance is exotic and ornamental, making it a striking addition to urban parks or botanical gardens.
description: A striking pink and maroon slipper-shaped flower with a spotted upper sepal and horizontally extended petals. Its unique pouch-like structure and bold coloration make it visually distinctive.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full pouch-like lower petal, spotted upper sepal, and both lateral petals extending outward.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the depth of the pouch and the arch of the upper sepal, with one lateral petal visible.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the upper sepal and the symmetrical spread of the lateral petals.
description_view_3: Opposite side view emphasizing the rounded pouch and the curvature of the upper sepal, with the other lateral petal in profile.
attribute_petal_arrangement: Bilateral arrangement with a prominent central pouch-like petal, two lateral petals extending horizontally, and a large dorsal sepal arching above; the structure is zygomorphic with clear left-right symmetry.
